name: Sierra de Hornachuelos – circular to the mirador del Santuario de los Ángeles
name_original: Sierra de Hornachuelos – circular al mirador del Santuario de los Ángeles
region_intro: Hiking around Hornachuelos offers access to the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park, a vast area of well-preserved Mediterranean forest within the Sierra Morena mountain range. The landscape is characterized by extensive *dehesas* of holm and cork oaks, alongside river valleys featuring poplars, ash trees, and willows. Rivers like the Bembézar and Guadiato have carved gorges through quartzite and slate, providing varied terrain and panoramic views. This region is part of a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, highlighting its ecological significance. Best hiking trails around Hornachuelos The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Hornachuelos – circular to the mirador del Santuario de los Ángeles, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views towards the Santuario de los Ángeles. Another top favourite among local hikers is Caño de Hierro Spring – Fuente los tres caños loop from Hornachuelos, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This trail includes the picturesque Fountain of the Three Spouts and diverse woodland. Local hikers also love the Carolinas Fountains Trail, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through varied natural scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Hornachuelos is defined by Mediterranean forests, *dehesas* with holm and cork oaks, and river gorges. The network offers options for easy family walks as well as more challenging routes with greater elevation gain. The routes in Hornachuelos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Hornachuelos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Hornachuelos?
A: There are nearly 40 hiking trails around Hornachuelos, offering a diverse range of options for all skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and some more challenging routes within the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Hornachuelos?
A: Yes, Hornachuelos offers several easy trails perfect for families and beginners. For example, the Botanical Path of Huerta del Rey is a 1.6 km family-friendly path that showcases the park's diverse plant life. Another gentle option is the 1.7 km circular Path of Rabilarga, which follows the Rabilarga stream and is suitable for all ages.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Hornachuelos?
A: The terrain in Hornachuelos is varied, characterized by extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; of holm and cork oaks, river valleys with lush vegetation, and impressive gorges carved through quartzite and slate. Some trails, like the Path of Las Herrerías, have rocky surfaces, while others follow sandy lanes. You can expect a mix of woodland paths, open pastureland, and some routes with moderate ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Hornachuelos?
A: Yes, Hornachuelos features several circular hiking routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995170792/sierra-de-hornachuelos-circular-to-the-mirador-del-santuario-de-los-angeles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Hornachuelos – circular to the mirador del Santuario de los Ángeles&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.6 km long. Another well-liked moderate loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24522815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caño de Hierro Spring – Fuente los tres caños loop from Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt;, covering 9.5 km and passing the picturesque Fountain of the Three Spouts.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking in Hornachuelos offers views of diverse natural features. You can see the impressive gorges carved by rivers like the Bembézar and Guadiato, and panoramic views of the Bembézar reservoir. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5340608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Huerta del Rey&lt;/a&gt; and the 'Mirador del Águila'. The region is also rich in Mediterranean flora, including holm and cork oaks, and various riverine species.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Hornachuelos?
A: The Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park is beautiful year-round, but autumn and spring are particularly pleasant for hiking due to milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Autumn also offers the chance to witness the 'berrea,' the red deer mating season. Winter can be a good option for cooler weather, while summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes more advisable.

Q: Is Hornachuelos a good place for wildlife spotting while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park is a haven for diverse wildlife. Hikers might spot wild boars, deer, and various bird species, including cinereous vultures, black storks, and golden eagles. It's particularly notable for hosting one of Spain's few tree-nesting Black vulture colonies and the largest colony of griffon vultures in Andalusia. Otters can also be found near watercourses.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several trails offer views of or access to water features. The region is known for its rivers like the Bembézar and Guadiato, which have carved impressive gorges. You can enjoy stunning views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt; and reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010075200/la-puebla-de-los-infantes-and-jose-toran-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puebla de los Infantes and José Torán Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a more challenging route that includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;José Torán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Hornachuelos?
A: The hiking routes in Hornachuelos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-preserved Mediterranean forests, the unique &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, and the varied terrain that offers both gentle walks and more challenging routes with rewarding views.

Q: Where can I find the starting points for hikes in Hornachuelos?
A: Many well-marked hiking and walking paths begin at the Visitors Centre Huerta del Rey, which is conveniently located just 1 km from Hornachuelos. This center serves as an excellent starting point for exploring the various trails within the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging hikes in the Hornachuelos area?
A: While many trails are easy to moderate, there are some more challenging options for experienced hikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010075200/la-puebla-de-los-infantes-and-jose-toran-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puebla de los Infantes and José Torán Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering nearly 20 km with significant elevation gain. The Sendero del Cordel del Águila also features medium difficulty with steep ascents and descents.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other outdoor activities in Hornachuelos?
A: Yes, Hornachuelos is an excellent base for various outdoor activities beyond hiking. You can enjoy canoeing on the calm waters of the Bembézar Reservoir, horse riding, climbing, and bike touring. For unique aerial perspectives, skydiving and hot-air balloon rides are also available. The area is also a designated Starlight Destination, perfect for stargazing.
tips: Population at the foot of the mountain range of the same name, in the Cordoba Sierra Morena. Its name comes from the Arabic "furnuyulu" (city of holes) and its layout is also of clear Arab influence with narrow and steep streets.
A little before reaching the Sanctuary of Santa María de los Ángeles (currently a residence for social reintegration) we found this viewpoint with excellent views of the Bembézar River.

waypoints.name: View of the Bembézar River from the Santuario de los Ángeles
